entities.name: GENOMIC MODELS
entities.description: Models developed for the analysis and application of large-crop plant genomes

entities.name: PLANT GENOME
entities.description: The total genetic material of a plant, which varies significantly in size across different species

entities.name: POLYPLOIDY
entities.description: A condition of having multiple sets of chromosomes, which contributes to variation in plant genome size

entities.name: DIPLOIDY
entities.description: A condition of having two complete sets of chromosomes

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A crop species with a DNA content of 0.50 pg, equivalent to 450 Mbp

entities.name: BARLEY
entities.description: A crop species with a DNA content of 5.55 pg, equivalent to 5300 Mbp

entities.name: GENE CONTENT
entities.description: The number of genes present in a genome, estimated to be under 40,000 for rice and barley

entities.name: GENE PREDICTION PROGRAM
entities.description: A computational tool used to estimate the number of genes in a genome

entities.name: DNA CONTENT
entities.description: The total amount of DNA in a genome, which varies by orders of magnitude between species

entities.name: NONGENIC DNA
entities.description: DNA sequences that do not code for proteins, making up much of the difference in DNA content between species

entities.name: RETROTRANSPOSONS
entities.description: A specific type of nongenic DNA that contributes to the variation in DNA content
